title: AutoScout24 scales engineering with AI-powered workflows
optimized_headline: AutoScout24 Enhances Engineering Efficiency with Innovative AI Workflows
raw_excerpt: Learn how AutoScout24 Group uses Codex and ChatGPT to speed development cycles, improve code quality, and expand AI adoption.
raw_body: Learn how AutoScout24 Group uses Codex and ChatGPT to speed development cycles, improve code quality, and expand AI adoption.
speedrun: AutoScout24 Group is enhancing its engineering processes by integrating AI tools like Codex and ChatGPT. This shift aims to accelerate development cycles and boost code quality. By leveraging these AI-powered workflows, the company is also promoting broader adoption of AI technologies within its teams. This matters now as companies increasingly seek efficiency and innovation in a competitive market.
why_it_matters: Developers at AutoScout24 will see faster project completion times, allowing them to focus on more complex tasks.
This move signifies a growing trend in the tech industry where AI tools are becoming essential for improving productivity and quality.

lenses.eli12: AutoScout24 is using AI tools like Codex and ChatGPT to help its engineers work faster and better. Imagine having a smart assistant that helps you write code, catching mistakes before they happen. This is important for everyday people because it could lead to better apps and services that we all use.
lenses.pm: For product managers and founders, AutoScout24's approach highlights a growing need for efficient development processes. By adopting AI tools, they can meet user demands more quickly while potentially reducing development costs. This could lead to faster product iterations and improved user experiences.
lenses.engineer: AutoScout24's integration of Codex and ChatGPT aims to streamline development workflows and enhance code quality. By utilizing these models, the company can reduce error rates and improve overall efficiency in coding tasks. This shift reflects a broader trend where AI is increasingly leveraged to optimize engineering practices.
